story.iWant: the Phase 1 database schema defined as EF Core entities with fluent API configurations
story.soThat: the application can persist and retrieve Google Calendar events, version history, save states, and app metadata locally
story.tasks: - Create 7 entity classes in Data/Entities/: GcalEvent, GcalEventVersion, SaveState, AuditLog, Config, DataSourceRefresh, SystemState — all properties matching _database-schemas.md column names - Create 7 fluent configuration classes in Data/Configurations/ implementing IEntityTypeConfiguration<T>: table names, column names, PKs, defaults, indexes, FK, and HasData seed for Config - Update CalendarDbContext.cs: add 7 DbSet properties, replace OnModelCreating placeholder with modelBuilder.ApplyConfigurationsFromAssembly(typeof(CalendarDbContext).Assembly) - Run dotnet ef migrations add Phase1Schema; review generated SQL for all 7 tables, indexes, FK, and seed data - Write integration tests in Tests/Integration/SchemaTests.cs: AllPhase1Tables_ExistAfterEnsureCreated, GcalEvent_CrudRoundTrip_Succeeds, GcalEventVersion_ForeignKey_EnforcedOnInsert, Config_SeedData_PresentAfterMigration - Final validation: build Debug+Release, inspect calendar.db with DB Browser, confirm __EFMigrationsHistory has InitialCreate + Phase1Schema, run full test suite

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Given Entity Framework Core configured from Story 1.2 When I create the Phase 1 entity model and apply the migration Then 7 tables are created in SQLite: gcal_event, gcal_event_version, save_state, audit_log, config, data_source_refresh, system_state

The entity model is correctly structured: - 7 C# entity classes in Data/Entities/ (no data annotations) - 7 fluent configuration classes in Data/Configurations/ - CalendarDbContext has 7 DbSet properties - OnModelCreating calls ApplyConfigurationsFromAssembly

The migration applies cleanly: - All 7 tables with correct columns, types, and defaults - All 9 indexes present: idx_gcal_event_date, idx_gcal_recurring, idx_gcal_source, idx_gcal_app_created, idx_version_event, idx_audit_timestamp, idx_audit_operation, idx_refresh_source, idx_refresh_date - FK: gcal_event_version.gcal_event_id → gcal_event.gcal_event_id - 6 default Config rows seeded

Schema is integration-tested: - All 7 tables exist after EnsureCreated() on in-memory SQLite - GcalEvent CRUD round-trip succeeds - GcalEventVersion FK constraint prevents orphan rows - Config seed data present (6 rows)

doc.title: Database Schema Reference
doc.section: Phase 1 Tables: gcal_event, gcal_event_version, save_state, audit_log, config, data_source_refresh, system_state
doc.snippet: Phase 1 (7 tables): gcal_event — Google Calendar event cache with app_created, source_system (Phase 3 nullable), gcal_etag; gcal_event_version — full snapshot for rollback with FK to gcal_event; save_state — snapshot_data JSON; audit_log — operation_type, user_action, success; config — config_key PK, config_type, HasData seed; data_source_refresh — source_name, start_date/end_date range, records_fetched; system_state — state_name UNIQUE, state_value.

doc.title: Database Schema Reference
doc.snippet: idx_gcal_event_date ON gcal_event(start_datetime, end_datetime); idx_gcal_recurring ON gcal_event(recurring_event_id); idx_gcal_source ON gcal_event(source_system); idx_gcal_app_created ON gcal_event(app_created).

doc.title: Database Schema Reference
doc.snippet: idx_version_event ON gcal_event_version(gcal_event_id, created_at DESC). FOREIGN KEY (gcal_event_id) REFERENCES gcal_event(gcal_event_id).

doc.title: System Architecture
doc.snippet: Entities in Data/Entities/ (14 classes for full schema). Configurations in Data/Configurations/ (one IEntityTypeConfiguration per entity). Repository pattern in Data/Repositories/. Migrations in Data/Migrations/. Story 1.3 adds Phase 1 entities only (7 of 14).

doc.title: System Architecture
doc.section: Decision Summary — Table Naming, Fluent API
doc.snippet: Table naming: singular (Key Decisions §3). Fluent API configuration — no data annotations. snake_case table and column names to match SQL schema doc.

doc.title: Epic Breakdown
doc.snippet: FR-8.1, NFR-D1, NFR-D4. EF Core fluent API for all config. EventDataJson stores full event state for rollback. Phase 1 schema only — additional tables in later epics. Consider DateTimeOffset for timezone handling.

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: FR-8.1: SQLite schema with full local storage. NFR-D1: Version history for rollback (gcal_event_version). NFR-D4: Audit trail for all operations (audit_log).

artifact.reason: Story 1.3 adds 7 DbSet properties and replaces the OnModelCreating placeholder comment with ApplyConfigurationsFromAssembly. Current file has no DbSets and a comment "populated in Story 1.3".
Story 1.3 integration tests reuse the same in-memory SQLite factory pattern established here. SchemaTests.cs should follow the same xUnit + FluentAssertions structure.
Reference pattern for xUnit test style: [Fact] methods, Arrange/Act/Assert, FluentAssertions .Should() syntax used across all test files.
EF Core 9.0.12 packages already present from Story 1.2. No new packages needed for Story 1.3.

package.name: Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Design
package.note: Required for dotnet ef migrations add

constraints.constraint: FLUENT API ONLY. No data annotations ([Required], [Column], [Key], [MaxLength]) on entity classes. All configuration via IEntityTypeConfiguration<T> classes in Data/Configurations/. This is an architecture mandate.

PHASE 1 SCOPE ONLY. Do not define entities for Phase 2 (PendingEvent) or Phase 3 (TogglData, YouTubeData, CallLogData, GeneratedEventSource, DateState, TrackedGap, WeeklyState). Those belong in future stories.

snake_case TABLE AND COLUMN NAMES. Every configuration class MUST call ToTable("snake_case_name") and HasColumnName("snake_case_col") for every property. EF Core defaults to PascalCase without explicit configuration.
STRING PK ENTITIES (GcalEvent, Config) must call .ValueGeneratedNever() on the primary key property. Without this, EF Core may attempt to generate string GUIDs automatically.

INTEGER AUTOINCREMENT PKs (GcalEventVersion, SaveState, AuditLog, DataSourceRefresh, SystemState) must use .ValueGeneratedOnAdd(). EF Core SQLite provider maps this to INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT.

source_system COLUMN on GcalEvent: This Phase 3 field must still be defined as a nullable string property on the entity. Deferring it creates a future breaking migration. Define it now, nullable, to match the schema doc.
UNIQUE CONSTRAINT on SystemState.StateName: Configure via builder.HasIndex(e => e.StateName).IsUnique() in SystemStateConfiguration.
FOREIGN KEY CASCADE: GcalEventVersion.GcalEventId has ON DELETE CASCADE — configure with .OnDelete(DeleteBehavior.Cascade) in GcalEventConfiguration navigation config.
CONFIG SEED DATA: HasData() in ConfigConfiguration must use static/fixed DateTime values (not DateTime.Now) to ensure the migration is deterministic and reproducible.

ApplyConfigurationsFromAssembly PLACEMENT: Call before base.OnModelCreating(). Configuration classes are discovered automatically if they implement IEntityTypeConfiguration<T> — no manual registration needed.

IN-MEMORY SQLITE FOR TESTS: Schema tests must use SQLite in-memory provider (Data Source=:memory:) with OpenConnection() + EnsureCreated(). The EF Core InMemory provider does not enforce FK constraints.

NULLABLE REFERENCE TYPES: All entity classes must be fully compatible with <Nullable>enable</Nullable>. Nullable columns → nullable C# type (string?). Required columns → non-nullable (string, not string?).

interface.name: CalendarDbContext — updated
IEntityTypeConfiguration<GcalEvent>
interface.signature: public class GcalEventConfiguration : IEntityTypeConfiguration<GcalEvent> { public void Configure(EntityTypeBuilder<GcalEvent> builder) { ... } }

interface.name: Config seed data (HasData)
interface.signature: builder.HasData(new Config { ConfigKey = "min_event_duration_minutes", ConfigValue = "5", ConfigType = "integer", Description = "...", UpdatedAt = new DateTime(2026, 1, 1) }, ...);

interface.name: Migration command
interface.path: Project root — requires Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Design in startup project

interface.name: SchemaTests in-memory factory
interface.signature: var options = new DbContextOptionsBuilder<CalendarDbContext>().UseSqlite("Data Source=:memory:").Options; var ctx = new CalendarDbContext(options); ctx.Database.OpenConnection(); ctx.Database.EnsureCreated(); return ctx;

tests.standards: Integration tests use xUnit with FluentAssertions. In-memory SQLite (Data Source=:memory:) with OpenConnection() + EnsureCreated() — NOT the EF Core InMemory provider (it does not enforce FK constraints). Each test creates and disposes its own CalendarDbContext. Pattern matches existing DatabaseInfrastructureTests.cs. New tests go in GoogleCalendarManagement.Tests/Integration/SchemaTests.cs. No Moq needed — real SQLite in-memory is preferred for schema-level tests.

ideas.idea: AllPhase1Tables_ExistAfterEnsureCreated — after EnsureCreated(), query "SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table'" and assert all 7 table names are present
GcalEvent_CrudRoundTrip_Succeeds — insert GcalEvent with a string PK, assert it reads back with matching properties, update summary, assert update persisted, delete, assert gone

GcalEventVersion_ForeignKey_EnforcedOnInsert — attempt to insert GcalEventVersion with a non-existent gcal_event_id; assert DbUpdateException (FK violation) is thrown. Requires real SQLite in-memory, not EF InMemory.
Config_SeedData_PresentAfterMigration — after MigrateAsync(), query Configs DbSet; assert Count() == 6, assert specific ConfigKey "min_event_duration_minutes" with value "5" exists
AllIndexes_PresentAfterMigration — query "SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='index'" and assert all 9 index names are present
